raw_text:
All searches are complete. Both broad and narrow queries returned no evidence for Subscriptions. I now have everything needed to compile the full commentary.

---

# VARIANCE COMMENTARY — FEBRUARY 2026

---

## EXECUTIVE SUMMARY

Three significant variances were identified in February 2026, totalling a net adverse position of **£253** against budget across the flagged lines. Two variances are adverse and fully explained by corroborated evidence; one is favourable and entirely unexplained after exhaustive search.

- **Advertising & Marketing** is over budget by **£667 (+66.7%)**, driven by a deliberate pull-forward of the Q2 spring campaign from March into February, MD-approved and formally documented.
- **Repairs & Maintenance** is over budget by **£786 (+786.3%)**, attributable to a single emergency HVAC failure on 14 February, CFO-approved, with invoice on file.
- **Subscriptions** is under budget by **£1,200 (-100.0%)** with zero spend recorded. No cancellation, deferral, or billing anomaly could be identified across Slack or Gmail. Finance review is recommended.

---

## LINE COMMENTARY

---

### 1. Advertising & Marketing
**Variance: +£667 adverse (+66.7%) | Budget: £1,000 | Actual: £1,667**

Actual spend exceeded budget by £667, arising from a deliberate acceleration of the Q2 spring campaign into February. Per Slack communications on 2026-02-03, the campaign team confirmed an early client approval and elected to capitalise on it, bringing forward the March spend plan (~£1,600 total commitment) into February. This decision was formally ratified by MD sign-off, documented in email on 2026-02-01, citing an agency recommendation linked to a weather-sensitive launch window. A budget reforecast note was noted as attached to that approval. Calendar evidence confirms the spring campaign kick-off was scheduled for 5 February, with the agency briefed on 1 February. The £1,667 actual aligns closely with the ~£1,600 commitment cited in Slack. **March Advertising & Marketing budget should be reviewed for corresponding relief, as this spend was pulled forward from that period.**

#### Sources
- Slack — 2024-11-23T12:15:00Z — slack-advertising_&_marketing-broad — *"marketing-slack-2026-02-03: Campaign team — moving the Q2 spring campaign launch to this month. Client approval came in early, let's capitalise. Total Feb commitment will be ~£1,600 vs the £1,000 March plan. Approvals from MD signed off."*
- Gmail — 2024-11-23T18:15:00Z — gmail-advertising_&_marketing-broad — *"gmail-2026-02-01-campaign-approval: MD approval for Q2 campaign acceleration. Spring campaign moved from March to February at agency recommendation — weather-sensitive launch window. Budget reforecast note attached."*
- Calendar — 2024-11-23T22:15:00Z — calendar-advertising_&_marketing-broad — *"cal-2026-02-01: Marketing launch meeting — Spring campaign kick-off moved to 5 February. Agency briefed on 1 February per Slack decision."*

---

### 2. Repairs & Maintenance
**Variance: +£786 adverse (+786.3%) | Budget: £100 | Actual: £886**

Actual spend exceeded budget by £786, entirely attributable to a single unplanned emergency event. On 14 February 2026, the HVAC unit in the server room failed overnight. The building manager engaged FastAir Engineering on an emergency basis; the resulting invoice totalled **£886.30**, inclusive of parts and emergency callout rates. CFO approval was obtained and documented on the same day. This is a one-off, non-recurring cost and no budget provision existed for an event of this nature. **No further action is required on this variance beyond confirming the invoice has been processed; management may wish to consider whether a contingency provision for emergency building works is appropriate in future budgets.**

#### Sources
- Slack — 2024-11-19T12:15:00Z — slack-repairs_maintenance-broad — *"ops-slack-2026-02-14: URGENT — HVAC unit in server room failed overnight. Building manager called emergency engineer. Quote came in at £886. CFO approved. Works completed today."*
- Gmail — 2024-11-19T18:15:00Z — gmail-repairs_maintenance-broad — *"gmail-2026-02-14-hvac-invoice: Invoice from FastAir Engineering — emergency HVAC repair 14 Feb 2026. £886.30 inclusive of parts and emergency callout rate. CFO approved same day."*

---

### 3. Subscriptions
**Variance: -£1,200 favourable (-100.0%) | Budget: £1,200 | Actual: £0**

Actual spend was nil against a budget of £1,200, representing a full underspend of £1,200. Despite broad and narrow searches across both Slack and Gmail — covering cancellation, deferral, missed billing, SaaS renewals, and software licences — **no supporting context was found to explain the absence of spend.** It is not possible to determine whether this reflects an intentional cancellation, a billing failure, a timing shift (e.g. invoiced outside the period), or an annual subscription that was expected monthly. **No context found — recommend review.** Finance should confirm with the relevant budget owner whether the subscription(s) have been cancelled, whether an invoice is outstanding, or whether the budget assumption is no longer valid.

#### Sources
*No evidence returned across four searches (broad and narrow, Slack and Gmail). Gap noted.*

---

## INSIGNIFICANT VARIANCES

The following lines recorded variances deemed immaterial and were not subject to detailed investigation. They are noted for completeness.

| Line Item | Variance | Direction |
|---|---|---|
| Sales | +£506 | +8.4% favourable |
| Purchases | -£500 | -100.0% favourable |
| Audit & Accountancy Fees | -£3 | -6.1% favourable |
| Bank Fees | -£15 | -50.0% favourable |
| Cleaning | -£1 | -0.8% favourable |
| Entertainment - 0% | -£50 | -100.0% favourable |
| Entertainment - 100% Business | +£13 | marginal adverse |
| General Expenses | -£254 | -211.7% favourable |
| Light, Power & Heating | +£13 | +14.9% adverse |
| Motor Vehicle Expenses | -£71 | -54.6% favourable |
| Postage, Freight & Courier | -£50 | -100.0% favourable |
| Printing & Stationery | -£20 | -100.0% favourable |
| Rent | -£46 | -4.4% favourable |
| Telephone & Internet | -£6 | -13.3% favourable |
| Travel National | -£150 | -100.0% favourable |

> *Note: General Expenses (-£254, -211.7%) sits at the boundary of significance by percentage; however given the relatively low absolute amount it has been retained in this section. Finance may wish to verify the budget baseline for this line.*

---
*Commentary prepared by DeltAgent | Period: February 2026 | All figures in GBP (£)*
